description: DayTrace MVP — сервис фиксации событий дня (Telegram Bot + Mini App + Web Admin UI). C# / Vue.js / PostgreSQL.

userStories.title: Solution scaffolding & project structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the .NET solution with proper project structure (API, Domain, Infrastructure, Bot, Tests) so that all subsequent stories have a clean codebase to work in.
acceptanceCriteria: ASP.NET Core Web API project created (DayTrace.Api)
Domain project (DayTrace.Domain) with entities, interfaces
Infrastructure project (DayTrace.Infrastructure) with EF Core, repositories
Bot project (DayTrace.Bot) for Telegram bot logic
Test project (DayTrace.Tests)
Docker Compose with PostgreSQL service
Solution builds without errors
.gitignore, Directory.Build.props configured
CORS middleware configured; allowed origins via env var ALLOWED_ORIGINS
Swagger/OpenAPI middleware configured; /swagger endpoint available in Development

Global exception handler middleware: all errors return {error: string, message: string} JSON; unhandled exceptions → 500 with error='internal_error'; no stack traces in response; logged with correlation ID via NLog
userStories.notes: Default stack: C# .NET 8+, PostgreSQL. No Serilog — use NLog.

userStories.title: NLog logging configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NLog configured for structured logging across all projects so that observability requirements (NFR-3) are met from day one.
acceptanceCriteria: NLog NuGet packages installed in all projects
nlog.config with console + file targets
Structured JSON logging for file target
Correlation ID middleware for request tracing; all log entries include correlation_id
Logger injection via DI works in controllers and services
Domain-level log points: reminder send attempts, period-job start/result/failure with job_id
userStories.notes: Always NLog per project preferences. NFR-3 observability.

userStories.title: PostgreSQL connection & EF Core setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need EF Core configured with PostgreSQL (Npgsql) so that I can create migrations and access the database.
acceptanceCriteria: Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL installed
DbContext created with connection string from configuration
Connection pooling configured
Health check endpoint for DB connectivity
EF Core migrations tooling works (dotnet ef migrations add/update)
appsettings.json + appsettings.Development.json with connection strings

userStories.title: Database migration: users, users_settings, events
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core tables (users, users_settings, events) created via EF Core migration so that user registration and event CRUD can be built.
acceptanceCriteria: users table: id (PK), telegram_user_id (unique), created_at, status
users_settings table: user_id (FK, unique), timezone, reminder_time, reminder_enabled, week_end

events table: id UUID PK (gen_random_uuid), user_id FK, text varchar(500) NOT NULL, local_date DATE NOT NULL, importance int NOT NULL CHECK(1..5), created_at timestamptz, updated_at timestamptz, deleted_at timestamptz NULL
Proper indexes on foreign keys and common query patterns
Migration applies and rolls back cleanly

userStories.title: Database migration: summaries, period_jobs, period_run_counters
userStories.description: As a developer, I need summary-related tables so that period summary generation infrastructure can be built.
acceptanceCriteria: summaries: id, user_id FK, period_type, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, status, version, content JSONB, source_event_ids UUID[], last_generated_at timestamptz; unique(user_id, period_type, period_start, period_end)

period_jobs: id, idempotency_key (unique), user_id, period_type, period_start, period_end, run_number, status, attempt_count default 0, lease_id UUID NULL, target_summary_version int, started_at, finished_at, error text, reconciled_at, recovery_source, created_at
period_run_counters: id, user_id, period_type, period_start, period_end, last_run_number; unique(user_id, period_type, period_start, period_end)
Migration applies cleanly

userStories.title: Database migration: history & delivery tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need week_schedule_history, timezone_history, delivery_attempts, and prompt_deliveries tables.
acceptanceCriteria: week_schedule_history: id, user_id FK, week_end enum, effective_from_local_date DATE, transition_start DATE NULL, transition_end DATE NULL, created_at; unique(user_id, effective_from_local_date)
timezone_history: id, user_id FK, timezone text NOT NULL, effective_from timestamptz NOT NULL, created_at timestamptz

delivery_attempts: id bigserial, user_id FK, delivery_type varchar, reference_id bigint NULL, attempt_number int default 1, status varchar, error_message text, telegram_message_id bigint NULL, scheduled_at timestamptz, sent_at timestamptz, created_at; indexes: (user_id, delivery_type, scheduled_at), partial (status) WHERE status IN ('pending','failed')
prompt_deliveries: id, prompt_id (unique), user_id FK, period_type, period_start, period_end, sent_at, channel, status; unique(user_id, period_type, period_start, period_end, sent_at)
Migration applies cleanly

userStories.title: Database migration: admin & operation_id_cache tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need admin_users, admin_sessions, audit_logs, and operation_id_cache tables.
acceptanceCriteria: admin_users: id, email (unique case-insensitive via unique(lower(email))), password_hash, role, status, created_at
admin_sessions: id, admin_user_id FK, token_hash, expires_at, created_at
audit_logs: id, actor_type, actor_id, action, target_type, target_id, payload JSONB, outcome, created_at
operation_id_cache: id, user_id, method, route, client_operation_id, response_hash, created_at; unique(user_id, method, route, client_operation_id)
Migration applies cleanly

userStories.title: Telegram Bot webhook setup & secret verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Telegram Bot webhook configured with secret token verification so that the bot can receive updates securely.
acceptanceCriteria: Telegram.Bot NuGet package installed
Webhook endpoint registered (POST /bot/webhook)
X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token verified on every request (FR-12.3)
Bot token stored in configuration (env/secrets, not code per NFR-4)
Invalid/missing secret token → 401
Webhook processes Update objects and dispatches to handlers

userStories.title: User registration & onboarding domain service
userStories.description: As a new user, I need to be registered automatically when I first interact with the service so that I can begin using it immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: UserRegistrationService.RegisterAsync(telegramUserId, detectedTimezone?) creates user + users_settings
Default settings: timezone=UTC (if no detected TZ), reminder_time=21:00, reminder_enabled=true, week_end=Sunday
When called with detected timezone (from Mini App) → persists detected timezone instead of UTC
Idempotent upsert by telegram_user_id — repeated calls do not create duplicates
Initial week_schedule_history record created with effective_from = registration_date - 31 days (FR-0)
Initial timezone_history record created (FR-0, FR-2.2)
Returns created/existing user with isNew flag
userStories.notes: Per FR-0. Pure domain service — bot UX and Mini App UX call this service. No bot-specific logic here.

userStories.title: Mini App auth handshake — POST /auth/telegram
userStories.description: As a Mini App user, I need to authenticate via Telegram init data so that my API requests are authorized.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/telegram accepts Telegram init data
Backend validates HMAC signature per Telegram docs
Evaluation order: auth_date max age check (300s) FIRST → then replay protection check
auth_date older than 300 seconds → 401 init_data_expired
On success: returns opaque session token (UUID) stored in sessions table
If user doesn't exist → calls UserRegistrationService (FR-0 flow) with detected timezone from init data
If user exists → returns token for existing user
userStories.notes: Per FR-12.1. Replay protection is separate story US-011. Token is opaque UUID (DB-backed), not JWT.

userStories.title: Replay protection for Mini App auth
userStories.description: As a security measure, I need replay protection on POST /auth/telegram so that intercepted init data cannot be reused maliciously.
acceptanceCriteria: Storage key = sha256(canonicalize(init_data_raw)), canonicalize = parse params, sort alphabetically, serialize
TTL = 300 seconds (matches auth_date max age)
First valid request stores key + session token result
Repeat request within TTL → idempotent response (same session token, 200)
After TTL expires + auth_date expired → 401
Concurrent requests: atomic set-if-not-exists, losers get winner's result

userStories.title: Session token management & auth middleware
userStories.description: As a user, I need my session token to work for 24 hours and auto-renew on activity so that I stay authenticated during normal usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Session token is opaque UUID; stored in sessions table with user_id, token_hash, expires_at, created_at
Auth middleware extracts token from Authorization: Bearer header
Middleware validates token via DB lookup (SELECT WHERE token_hash = ? AND expires_at > now())
Invalid/expired/missing token → 401 unauthorized
On successful validation: UPDATE expires_at = now() + 24h (sliding window renewal)
Middleware sets current user context (user_id, timezone) for downstream handlers
userStories.notes: Per FR-12.1 session, FR-12.2. Opaque token + DB lookup — simpler than JWT for sliding expiration.

userStories.title: POST /events — create event with validation
userStories.description: As a user, I need to create a day event with text, importance, and optional local_date so that I can record what happened.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /events accepts {text, importance, local_date?}
text: 1..500 chars, required → 400 validation_error if invalid
importance: 1..5 int, required → 400 validation_error if invalid
local_date: YYYY-MM-DD, optional (default = today in user TZ via DateCalculationService), must be within [today-30, today] → 400 date_out_of_range
Returns 201 with {id, text, importance, local_date, created_at}
event.id is UUID (gen_random_uuid)
X-Client-Operation-Id header required for dedupe (POST only)

userStories.title: GET /events — list events with filtering & pagination
userStories.description: As a user, I need to list my events filtered by date range with cursor pagination.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /events with query params: from, to (local_date filter), limit (1..100, default 20), cursor
Default filter: current day in user TZ
Returns only non-deleted events (deleted_at IS NULL) for current user
Sorted by local_date DESC, created_at DESC
Cursor-based pagination (opaque base64 cursor)
Response: {items: [...], next_cursor: string|null}

userStories.title: PATCH /events/{id} — edit event
userStories.description: As a user, I need to edit my event's text and importance within 7 days of creation.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /events/{id} accepts {text?, importance?} — partial update
Only text and importance are editable; local_date is immutable (FR-1)
Edit allowed only within 168 hours of created_at → 422 edit_window_expired if expired
Validates same rules as creation (text 1..500, importance 1..5)
Updates updated_at timestamp
404 if event not found or belongs to another user
404 if event is soft-deleted
userStories.notes: Per FR-1. 422 for business rule violation (edit window), not 403.

userStories.title: DELETE /events/{id} — soft delete event
userStories.description: As a user, I need to delete my event within 7 days of creation (soft delete).
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /events/{id} sets deleted_at = now()
Only allowed within 168 hours of created_at → 422 edit_window_expired if expired
404 if event not found or belongs to another user
Already-deleted event → 404
Deleted events excluded from all queries and summary generation
userStories.notes: Per FR-1 soft-delete. 422 for business rule violation.

userStories.title: Client operation ID dedupe middleware
userStories.description: As a system, I need to deduplicate requests by X-Client-Operation-Id header to prevent double-submits.
acceptanceCriteria: Middleware reads X-Client-Operation-Id from request header
Applies only to POST, PATCH, DELETE endpoints; GET requests bypass dedupe
Unique key = (user_id, method, route, client_operation_id) per section 4.1
First request: stores result hash, proceeds normally
Duplicate within 5 min TTL → returns cached response (200 idempotent)
Atomic insert with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
Periodic cleanup of expired entries: batch DELETE with LIMIT to avoid long locks, runs every minute, logs count of cleaned entries
Missing header on POST/PATCH/DELETE → 400
userStories.notes: Per FR-9, section 4.1 operation_id_cache. Absorbs US-061 (cleanup job).

userStories.title: GET /settings — read user settings
userStories.description: As a user, I need to read my current settings (timezone, reminder_time, reminder_enabled, week_end).
acceptanceCriteria: GET /settings returns full settings object for authenticated user
Response: {timezone, reminder_time, reminder_enabled, week_end}
401 if not authenticated

userStories.title: PUT /settings — update basic settings (reminder_time, reminder_enabled)
userStories.description: As a user, I need to update my reminder time and enabled flag.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /settings accepts partial update: {reminder_time?, reminder_enabled?}
reminder_time: HH:mm format, 24h, no seconds → 400 validation_error if invalid
reminder_enabled: boolean
Only provided fields are updated, others unchanged
Returns full updated settings object
401 if not authenticated
userStories.notes: Per FR-3, FR-13.1. Timezone and week_end changes are separate stories due to complexity.

userStories.title: PUT /settings — timezone change with cooldown & history
userStories.description: As a user, I need to change my timezone with a 24-hour cooldown and proper history tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /settings with {timezone} validates IANA timezone string
Rate limit: max 1 change per 24 hours → 429 timezone_change_cooldown with retry_after_seconds
On change: creates timezone_history record (FR-2.2)
Reminder schedule recalculated immediately in new TZ
If next scheduled reminder in new TZ is already past → skipped (no retroactive send)
Closed periods (generated/failed) not recalculated
Open weekly period boundaries recalculated per week_schedule_history and new TZ; if today_local in new TZ pushes week_end into past → no retroactive auto-trigger
Open monthly/yearly period boundaries recalculated in new TZ
today_local recalculated in new TZ for backdate window
When both timezone and week_end present in same request → timezone applied first, then week_end transition computed from new today_local (FR-13.1)

userStories.title: PUT /settings — week_end change with transition period
userStories.description: As a user, I need to change my week_end day, which creates a transition period of variable length.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /settings with {week_end} triggers compute_transition(old, new, change_date) per FR-4.3
Same week_end as current → no-op, no transition
409 transition_pending if previous transition not resolved; response includes transition_start, transition_end, hint
Transition period: 1-7 days, stored in week_schedule_history with transition_start/transition_end
New week_schedule_history record with effective_from_local_date = first_new_week_start
If transition already in past (catch-up): auto-creates period_job if non-deleted events exist in transition period

Empty catch-up transition (0 non-deleted events in [transition_start, transition_end]): no period_job created, no summary record. Does NOT block future week_end changes (409 transition_pending check passes)
Transition completion check: summary.status == 'generated' OR count of non-deleted events in transition period == 0
userStories.notes: Per FR-4.3, FR-4.4. One of the most complex features.

userStories.title: Timezone-aware date calculations service
userStories.description: As a system, I need a centralized service for all timezone-aware date calculations (today_local, period boundaries, DST).
acceptanceCriteria: Service computes today_local for any IANA timezone
Week boundaries: week_start/week_end from week_schedule_history
Month boundaries: first/last day of month in user TZ
Year boundaries: Jan 1 / Dec 31 in user TZ
Handles DST transitions correctly
All period boundaries are inclusive DATE ranges (FR-2.1)
Fallback rule: if local_date < min(effective_from_local_date) for user → use earliest week_schedule_history record (FR-4.4)
Unit tests for DST edge cases, year boundaries, week boundaries, fallback rule
userStories.notes: Per FR-2, FR-2.1, FR-4.4 fallback. Foundation for all period calculations. Must be implemented before event CRUD.

userStories.title: Period run counters & idempotency key management
userStories.description: As a system, I need atomic period run counter management and idempotency key generation for period jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: Idempotency key = hash(user_id, period_type, period_start, period_end, run_number)
First access: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING with last_run_number = 1
Idempotent trigger: SELECT last_run_number (no increment)
Force re-run: atomic UPDATE last_run_number + 1 RETURNING
Terminal fail recovery: conditional increment when existing job is terminal failed (attempt_count >= 3, status = failed)
All operations are atomic (single SQL statement or transaction)
Idempotency keys retained in DB for minimum 180 days (FR-8.1)

userStories.title: Period job creation transaction
userStories.description: As a system, I need the full period job creation transaction (section 4.4.1) including lock acquisition, run_number handling, event count check, and summary creation.
acceptanceCriteria: Lock order: counters → events check → jobs → summaries (prevents deadlocks)
Auto-trigger mode: SELECT last_run_number without increment; if 0 non-deleted events in period → ROLLBACK, no period_job created
Auto-trigger mode: if summary already exists with status=generated → ROLLBACK, return 'already_generated'
Auto-trigger mode: terminal fail recovery — if existing job has status=failed AND attempt_count >= 3 → atomically increment run_number, create new job with new idempotency_key
Force re-run mode: atomic UPDATE last_run_number + 1; creates new period_job with new key
Force re-run mode with existing summary → UPDATE status='generating', version++; gets target_version
Force re-run mode with no existing summary and 0 events → 400 empty_period (no summary/job created)
ON CONFLICT (idempotency_key) returns existing job without creating duplicate
Summary created/updated with status='generating' at job creation time; target_summary_version recorded in period_job
userStories.notes: Per section 4.4.1 pseudocode. Single transaction with mode parameter, not separate implementations.

userStories.title: Period job worker: claim, execute, finalize
userStories.description: As a system, I need a background worker that claims pending/retried jobs, generates summary content, and finalizes with proper fencing.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker polls for jobs in status pending/retried (SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED)
TX1: Claim job — set status=running, lease_id=new UUID, attempt_count=attempt_count+1
Partial success recovery: if summary already has status=generated for target version → mark job success, skip generation
Summary status recovery: if summary status=failed → UPDATE to generating (fenced by version + lease_id JOIN)
Work phase: call summary generation service (FR-14.1)
TX2: Finalize — UPDATE summary SET status=generated, content, source_event_ids (fenced by version + lease_id + status=generating); if 0 rows affected → mark job superseded
On non-timeout worker failure: summary remains with status=failed, content=NULL (distinguishable from 'never processed' = no record)
Worker UPDATE includes WHERE lease_id = ?; if 0 rows → job status=superseded
Worker runs as hosted service with configurable polling interval

userStories.title: Summary generation algorithm (FR-14.1)
userStories.description: As a system, I need to generate summary content as a structured JSON with sorted events for a given period.
acceptanceCriteria: Select non-deleted events where local_date in [period_start, period_end]
Sort: local_date ASC, importance DESC, created_at ASC
Generate content JSON: {events: [{event_id, text, importance, local_date}], total_events, period_start, period_end}
source_event_ids = array of all included event UUIDs
Returns content + source_event_ids for worker to persist
userStories.notes: Per FR-14.1. No AI summarization in MVP.

userStories.title: Weekly auto-trigger on event creation
userStories.description: As a system, I need to auto-trigger weekly summary generation when a user creates an event on week_end day.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful POST /events, check if today_local == week_end for user
Check saved event's local_date falls within [period_start, period_end] of target week
Backdated events outside target period do NOT trigger
Only on POST (create), not PATCH or DELETE
Check at least 1 non-deleted event exists in period (same transaction, step 3)
If generated summary already exists → no new job
Creates period_job via auto_trigger mode (FR-8.2a); once per run_number only
Terminal failure recovery: if existing job has terminal failed status → increment run_number, create new job (FR-8.2a)
Records prompt_delivery with channel=auto
Event and period_job in separate transactions (event saved independently)

userStories.title: Monthly auto-trigger on event creation
userStories.description: As a system, I need to auto-trigger monthly summary generation on the last day of the month.
acceptanceCriteria: After POST /events, check if today_local is last day of calendar month in user TZ
Saved event's local_date must be in target month's [first_day, last_day]
Same rules as weekly: backdated events outside target → no trigger
Creates period_job via auto_trigger mode; once per run_number only (FR-8.2a)
Terminal failure recovery: if existing job terminal failed → increment run_number, new job
If no events created on last day → soft reminder about manual run (delivered via delivery_attempts with type=soft_reminder)
Records prompt_delivery with channel=auto
userStories.notes: Per FR-6. Reuses PeriodAutoTriggerService with periodType=monthly.

userStories.title: Yearly auto-trigger on event creation
userStories.description: As a system, I need to auto-trigger yearly summary generation on December 31st.
acceptanceCriteria: After POST /events, check if today_local is Dec 31 in user TZ
Saved event's local_date must be in target year [Jan 1, Dec 31]
Same trigger rules as weekly/monthly; once per run_number only (FR-8.2a)
Terminal failure recovery: if existing job terminal failed → increment run_number, new job
If no events created on Dec 31 → soft reminder about manual run (delivered via delivery_attempts with type=soft_reminder)
Records prompt_delivery with channel=auto
userStories.notes: Per FR-7. Reuses PeriodAutoTriggerService with periodType=yearly.

userStories.title: Terminal fail recovery for auto-triggers
userStories.description: As a system, I need auto-triggers to recover from terminal failures by incrementing run_number and creating new jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: When auto-trigger finds existing job with status=failed AND attempt_count >= 3
Atomically increment last_run_number in period_run_counters
Create new period_job with new idempotency_key
Mark old terminal failed job: reconciled_at = now(), recovery_source = 'auto_trigger'
Prevents duplicate recovery (reconciliation won't re-trigger)
Works for weekly, monthly, and yearly triggers

userStories.title: POST /summaries/{periodType}/run — manual run
userStories.description: As a user, I need to manually trigger summary generation for any period type (weekly/monthly/yearly).
acceptanceCriteria: POST /summaries/{periodType}/run with optional {period_start, period_end}
X-Client-Operation-Id required (dedupe)
periodType ∈ {weekly, monthly, yearly} → 400 invalid_period if not
400 invalid_period for malformed/inconsistent dates
Force re-run mode: increments run_number (FR-8.2b)
Returns {job_id, period_type, period_start, period_end, run_number, status, summary_id}
400 empty_period if no events and no existing summary
Upsert: existing summary → version++, content recalculated
Manual run for transition periods always allowed (no 409)
Dedupe: repeat X-Client-Operation-Id within 5 min → 200 with identical response body

userStories.title: Deterministic period selection for manual run
userStories.description: As a system, I need to correctly select the target period when manual run is called without explicit dates.
acceptanceCriteria: Weekly: last fully completed weekly period (today_local > period_end), including transition periods
Exception: if today == period_end and auto-trigger already created summary (status ∈ {generating, generated}) → period is 'completed' for manual run
Monthly: last fully completed calendar month
Yearly: last fully completed calendar year
Uses week_schedule_history to compute weekly boundaries
Deterministic: same input always → same period
Unit tests for edge cases: today=period_end with/without existing summary

userStories.title: GET /summaries/{periodType} — list summaries with pagination
userStories.description: As a user, I need to list my summaries for a given period type with date filtering and cursor pagination.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /summaries/{periodType} with query: from, to, limit (1..100, default 20), cursor
Filters by period_start >= from AND period_end <= to
Sort order: period_start DESC, period_end DESC
Cursor encodes last seen period_start + period_end for stable pagination
Returns summaries in all statuses (generated, generating, failed); status field included in response
Response: {items: [summary objects per FR-14], next_cursor: string|null}
userStories.notes: Per FR-13.1. All statuses returned so UI can show in-progress/error states.

userStories.title: Summary upsert & empty period handling (FR-14.2, FR-14.3)
userStories.description: As a system, I need correct upsert behavior for summaries and proper handling of empty periods.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeat generation of same period: version++, last_generated_at updated, content & source_event_ids recalculated
Empty period + no existing summary → 400 empty_period (no summary created)
Empty period + existing summary (events deleted after generation) → force re-run allowed, content = {events:[], total_events:0}, source_event_ids = []
Auto-trigger with 0 events → does not trigger (FR-4/6/7)
Unique constraint on summaries prevents duplicates

userStories.title: Stuck job reaper with lease_id fencing
userStories.description: As a system, I need a background reaper that detects stuck jobs (>5 min) and marks them failed with proper fencing.
acceptanceCriteria: Cron job runs every 2 minutes
Detects jobs: status=running AND started_at < now() - 5 minutes
Sets status=failed, finished_at=now(), lease_id=NULL, error='timeout: exceeded 5 min'
Updates related summary to status=failed (fenced by target_summary_version — only if version matches)
Zombie worker protection: worker's subsequent updates fail due to NULL lease_id
Triple fencing: lease_id on job, lease_id JOIN on summary, optimistic version

userStories.title: Retry processor with exponential backoff
userStories.description: As a system, I need a retry processor that re-queues failed jobs with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts).
acceptanceCriteria: Combined with reaper cron (every 2 min)
Selects failed jobs where attempt_count < 3 AND backoff elapsed
Backoff: 30s * 2^(attempt_count-1) → 30s after 1st, 60s after 2nd
Sets status=retried, lease_id=NULL (worker picks up retried jobs)
FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for safe concurrent processing
Max 10 jobs per cron cycle
After 3 attempts: terminal failure (no more retries)

userStories.title: Terminal failure reconciliation
userStories.description: As a system, I need reconciliation to auto-recover terminally failed jobs that can't be recovered by auto-triggers.
acceptanceCriteria: Combined with reaper cron (every 2 min)
Selects terminal failed jobs: status=failed, attempt_count >= 3, finished_at < now()-5min, reconciled_at IS NULL
Precondition 1: no newer period_job with higher run_number exists
Precondition 2: summary not already generated with version >= target
If preconditions pass: increment run_number, create new job, set summary=generating
Mark old job: reconciled_at=now(), recovery_source='reconciliation'
If preconditions fail: only set reconciled_at=now() (no new job)
Covers catch-up transition periods (FR-4.3)

userStories.title: Daily reminder scheduler
userStories.description: As a user, I need to receive daily reminders at my configured time in my timezone.
acceptanceCriteria: Background service schedules reminders per user: reminder_time in user TZ
Only sends if reminder_enabled = true
Sends Telegram message via Bot API
Delivery SLA: ±5 minutes (NFR-1)
Logs actual UTC timestamp for audit
Skips if next reminder in past after TZ change (no retroactive send)
Period-end soft reminders: if auto-trigger did not fire on week_end/last day of month/Dec 31, send soft reminder next day suggesting manual run (delivery_type=soft_reminder)
userStories.notes: Per FR-3, NFR-1. Also handles soft reminders from FR-6/FR-7.

userStories.title: DST handling for reminders
userStories.description: As a system, I need correct reminder behavior during DST transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Spring-forward (time doesn't exist): shift to next valid local time
Fall-back (time occurs twice): send only on first occurrence
Scheduler stores and logs actual UTC timestamp
Unit tests for spring-forward and fall-back scenarios
Works with any IANA timezone

userStories.title: Delivery attempts tracking with retry policy
userStories.description: As a system, I need to track all Telegram message delivery attempts with retry on transient errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Each delivery attempt recorded in delivery_attempts table
delivery_type: reminder | summary_notification | soft_reminder
Retry on transient errors (network, 429, 5xx): up to 5 attempts with exponential backoff
Terminal errors (4xx except 429): immediate terminal_failed status
telegram_message_id stored on successful send
Partial index on (status) WHERE status IN ('pending', 'failed') for retry processor

userStories.title: Prompt delivery tracking
userStories.description: As a system, I need to track when summary generation prompts are sent for metrics calculation.
acceptanceCriteria: prompt_deliveries record created for each auto-trigger and manual run
Fields: prompt_id, user_id, period_type, period_start, period_end, sent_at, channel, status
channel = 'auto' for auto-triggers, 'manual' for manual runs
Unique constraint on (user_id, period_type, period_start, period_end, sent_at)
Used as source for Prompt→Summary conversion metric (METRICS.md)

userStories.title: Telegram Bot — /start command & quick action menu
userStories.description: As a user, I need bot commands and inline keyboards for quick actions.
acceptanceCriteria: /start → calls UserRegistrationService; if new user, sends welcome message
Welcome message includes one-time timezone hint if timezone=UTC: 'Откройте Mini App, чтобы мы определили ваш часовой пояс'
Quick action inline keyboard: [Add Event] [Weekly] [Monthly] [Yearly] [Mini App] [Settings]
Bot responds to unrecognized text with help message
Command handlers: /start, /help, /settings
Inline buttons route to appropriate handlers
userStories.notes: Per FR-9, FR-10. Registration logic in US-009 service; this is bot UX only.

userStories.title: Telegram Bot — event creation with importance selection
userStories.description: As a user, I need to create events via Bot with inline star buttons for importance.
acceptanceCriteria: User sends text → bot asks for importance via inline keyboard (★ to ★★★★★)
After importance selected → event created with today's local_date
Confirmation message with event details
Option to set custom local_date (backdate) via follow-up prompt
Anti-double-submit: repeated clicks within 3 seconds ignored (FR-9)

userStories.title: Telegram Bot — manual summary run
userStories.description: As a user, I need to trigger summary generation via Bot buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Inline buttons for [Generate Weekly] [Generate Monthly] [Generate Yearly]
Calls POST /summaries/{periodType}/run internally
Shows progress: 'Формируем...' → 'Готово: N событий за период X–Y' or 'Ошибка: [error.message]'
Anti-double-submit: repeated clicks within 3 seconds ignored
Error messages: 'В периоде нет событий' (empty_period), 'Некорректный период' (invalid_period)

userStories.title: Telegram Bot — settings management
userStories.description: As a user, I need to view and change basic settings via Bot.
acceptanceCriteria: /settings command shows current settings
Inline buttons: [Change Timezone] [Change Reminder Time] [Toggle Reminder] [Change Week End]
Each change triggers PUT /settings flow
Timezone: shows hint to use Mini App for auto-detection
Confirmation after each change

userStories.title: Mini App — Vue.js SPA shell with Telegram SDK
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Mini App scaffolding with Telegram SDK integration, auth flow, and navigation so that screen stories have a working foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Vue.js 3 project created (Vite + Vue 3 + Vue Router + Pinia)
Telegram Mini App SDK integrated (@telegram-apps/sdk or @tma.js/sdk)
Auth flow: on open → extract init data → POST /auth/telegram → store session token in Pinia
Timezone auto-detection: Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone sent with auth request
Navigation: bottom tabs or sidebar for 5 screens (Today, Week, Month, Year, Settings)
Telegram theme integration: colors from themeParams, viewport handling, back button
API client (axios/fetch wrapper) with automatic Bearer token injection
Loading and error states for auth flow
Project builds without errors
userStories.notes: Foundation for US-046..050. Must be implemented before any screen stories.

userStories.title: Mini App — Today screen (event creation & management)
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Today screen in Mini App to view today's events, add new ones, and edit/delete recent events.
acceptanceCriteria: Displays list of today's events (non-deleted), sorted by importance DESC
Add event form: text input (max 500 chars, counter shown), importance stars (1-5)
Optional date picker for backdating (within 30 days)
Edit/delete buttons visible only for events within 7-day window
Edit: inline text/importance editing with save/cancel
Delete: confirmation dialog before soft-delete
After create/edit/delete: list refreshes automatically

userStories.title: Mini App — Week screen
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Week screen to view weekly events grouped by day and generate/view weekly summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows events for current/selected week grouped by local_date
Each event shows text, importance (stars), date
Summary section: shows generated summary content, 'Формируется...' for generating, 'Ошибка' for failed, or 'Нет summary' if no record
[Generate Weekly Summary] button → calls manual run API
Week navigation: previous/next week
Transition periods displayed correctly (may have non-standard length)

userStories.title: Mini App — Month screen
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Month screen to view monthly events and generate/view monthly summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows events for current/selected month grouped by local_date
Calendar or list view of events with importance indicators
Summary section: generated monthly summary content or status (generating/failed/none)
[Generate Monthly Summary] button
Month navigation: previous/next month

userStories.title: Mini App — Year screen
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Year screen to view yearly events and generate/view yearly summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows events for current/selected year, grouped by month then day
Summary section: generated yearly summary content or status
[Generate Yearly Summary] button
Year navigation: previous/next year
Event count per month visualization

userStories.title: Mini App — Settings screen
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Settings screen to manage timezone, reminder, and week_end preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows current settings: timezone, reminder_time, reminder_enabled, week_end
Timezone: auto-detected dropdown with manual override, shows cooldown status if recently changed
Reminder time: time picker (HH:mm)
Reminder enabled: toggle switch
Week end: day-of-week selector with transition period warning if applicable
Save button calls PUT /settings
Error handling: shows 409 transition_pending message with transition dates, 429 cooldown with retry time

userStories.title: Admin auth — POST /admin/auth/login
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to authenticate via email+password to access Admin UI.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /admin/auth/login accepts {email, password}
Validates credentials against admin_users (bcrypt password_hash)
Returns admin session token with role in payload
Session TTL 8 hours, no auto-renew
Login event recorded in audit_logs
Invalid credentials → 401
Case-insensitive email matching

userStories.title: Admin RBAC middleware & role enforcement
userStories.description: As a system, I need RBAC middleware that enforces admin/operator/analyst roles on admin API endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Middleware extracts role from admin session token
Role hierarchy: admin > operator > analyst
admin: full access
operator: read-only to event texts, no deletion
analyst: only aggregated metrics, no PII/event texts (NFR-4)
Missing/invalid token → 401, insufficient role → 403
Logout event recorded in audit_logs

userStories.title: Admin seed script for initial admin user
userStories.description: As a deployer, I need a seed script to create the initial admin user since there's no self-registration.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI command or migration seed to create admin user
Accepts email and password (or generates random password)
Sets role = admin
Idempotent: re-running doesn't create duplicates
Documents usage in README
userStories.notes: Per FR-12.4: roles assigned via DB or admin seed.

userStories.title: Admin API — Dashboard metrics endpoint
userStories.description: As an admin/analyst, I need GET /metrics/dashboard returning aggregated metrics per METRICS.md formulas.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /metrics/dashboard endpoint (admin auth required, analyst role minimum)
DAU: count distinct user_id with events.created_at in target day
WAU: count distinct user_id with events in last 7 days (sliding window)
MAU: count distinct user_id with events in last 30 days (sliding window)
Reminder→Event conversion: converted_reminders / sent_reminders (24h conversion window)
Prompt→Summary conversion: successful_summaries / sent_prompts (48h window)
All formulas MUST match METRICS.md exactly (single source of truth)
Response includes timestamp of last calculation
userStories.notes: Per FR-11, METRICS.md. Backend API only; frontend is US-059 (Admin SPA).

userStories.title: Admin API — Users management endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin/operator, I need API endpoints to view user profiles, settings, and last activity.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/users — user list with search/filter, pagination
GET /admin/users/{id} — user detail: telegram_user_id, settings, registration date, last activity
Shows current timezone, reminder config, week_end
Event count and summary stats per user
Operator: read-only access
All actions logged in audit_logs

userStories.title: Admin API — Content management endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin, I need API endpoints to browse user events and summaries with role-based PII access.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/events — events list filterable by user, date range, importance; paginated
GET /admin/summaries — summaries list filterable by user, period type, date range, status; paginated
Admin role: full access to event text
Operator role: read-only access to event text
Analyst role: no access to event texts (403 or redacted fields per NFR-4)
Admin actions logged in audit_logs

userStories.title: Admin API — Operations endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin/operator, I need API endpoints to monitor delivery attempts, period job statuses, and errors.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/period-jobs — period jobs list: status, attempts, timing, errors; filterable/paginated
GET /admin/delivery-attempts — delivery attempts: status, retry count, scheduled vs actual times; filterable/paginated
Filter by status (failed, pending, running)
Error details visible for debugging
Stuck job indicators (running > 5 min)
Correlation/job ID displayed for tracing (NFR-3)

userStories.title: Admin API — Audit log endpoint
userStories.description: As an admin, I need an API endpoint to view the audit log of all admin actions.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/audit-logs — audit log list: actor, action, target, timestamp, outcome; paginated
Filterable by actor, action type, date range
Includes login/logout events
Only admin role can view full audit log
userStories.notes: Per FR-11, NFR-4. 180-day retention cleanup is US-062.

userStories.title: Admin UI — Vue.js SPA shell & pages
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Admin UI as a Vue.js SPA with routing, auth, and layout, rendering data from admin API endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Vue.js 3 project with Vue Router (Vite)
Login page → auth flow → dashboard
Sidebar navigation: Dashboard, Users, Content, Operations, Audit
Role-based menu visibility (analyst sees only Dashboard)
Dashboard page: renders metrics from GET /metrics/dashboard, auto-refresh every 15 min
Users page: renders user list/detail from admin API
Content page: events + summaries from admin API
Operations page: period jobs + delivery attempts
Audit page: audit logs
Responsive layout
API client with admin token management
userStories.notes: Admin UI frontend. Depends on all admin API stories.

userStories.title: User soft-delete and hard-delete PII purge
userStories.description: As a system, I need to support user deletion with soft-delete immediately and hard-delete PII within 30 days.
acceptanceCriteria: Soft-delete: sets users.status = 'deleted' immediately; user can no longer authenticate or create events
Hard-delete purge job: background job runs daily, processes users where status='deleted' AND deleted_at < now()-30 days

Purge order per NFR-7: period_jobs → summaries → events → prompt_deliveries (anonymize: user_id=NULL) → audit_logs (anonymize: actor_id=NULL, payload='{}') → delivery_attempts → FK-deps → users_settings → users
Post-purge SQL invariants: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events/summaries/users WHERE user_id=? → 0
Idempotent: partial failure → safe retry on next run

userStories.title: Audit log retention cleanup
userStories.description: As a system, I need automatic cleanup of audit log entries older than 180 days.
acceptanceCriteria: Background job runs daily
Deletes audit_logs entries where created_at < now() - 180 days
Batch deletion to avoid lock contention
Logs number of purged entries

userStories.title: Dockerfile & deployment configuration
userStories.description: As a deployer, I need Docker images for API+Bot and Mini App/Admin UI with proper configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Multi-stage Dockerfile for .NET API+Bot (build + runtime)
Dockerfile for Vue.js frontends (build + nginx)
Docker Compose for full stack (API, Bot, PostgreSQL, frontends)
Environment variables for all secrets (bot token, DB connection, JWT secret)
Health check endpoints configured
README with deployment instructions

userStories.title: Integration tests — event lifecycle
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests covering the full event lifecycle: create, read, edit, delete.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: create event with valid data → 201
Test: create event with invalid text/importance → 400
Test: create event with out-of-range date → 400
Test: edit event within 7 days → success
Test: edit event after 7 days → 422
Test: soft delete → event excluded from queries
Test: dedupe via client_operation_id
Tests run against real PostgreSQL (Docker)

userStories.title: Integration tests — summary generation & concurrency
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests covering summary generation, auto-triggers, manual runs, and concurrency guarantees.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: auto-trigger creates job on week_end with qualifying event
Test: auto-trigger does NOT fire for backdated events outside target period
Test: manual run creates/updates summary (upsert)
Test: concurrent auto-triggers → only one job created (idempotency)
Test: terminal failure → recovery creates new job
Test: stuck job reaper marks running job as failed after timeout
Test: retry processor re-queues failed jobs with backoff
Tests run against real PostgreSQL

userStories.title: Integration tests — auth, timezone, week_end change
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests for auth flows, timezone edge cases, and week_end transition periods.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: Mini App auth with valid init data → session token
Test: expired init data → 401
Test: replay protection → idempotent response within TTL
Test: timezone change cooldown → 429
Test: week_end change creates correct transition period
Test: 409 transition_pending when previous transition not resolved
Test: DST spring-forward and fall-back for reminders
Test: deterministic period selection (FR-5 rules)
